PWD.used_for: To reference the current directory in a script

OLDPWD.used_for: To reference the previous directory in a script

HOME.used_for: To reference the user's home directory in a script

USER.description: The username of the current user
USER.used_for: To reference the username in a script

HOSTNAME.used_for: To reference the hostname in a script

SHELL.description: The path to the current user's shell
SHELL.used_for: To reference the user's shell in a script

PATH.description: The list of directories where executable files are located
PATH.used_for: To specify the search path for executable files in a script

TERM.description: The type of terminal being used
TERM.used_for: To determine the terminal type in a script

LANG.used_for: To determine the language and character encoding settings in a script

RANDOM.used_for: To generate random numbers in a script

UID.description: The user ID of the current user
UID.used_for: To reference the user ID in a script

EUID.description: The effective user ID of the current user
EUID.used_for: To reference the effective user ID in a script

BASH_VERSION.description: The version number of the Bash shell
BASH_VERSION.used_for: To check the version of Bash in a script

BASH.description: The path to the Bash executable
BASH.used_for: To specify the Bash executable in a script

BASH_VERSINFO.description: An array containing version information for Bash
BASH_VERSINFO.used_for: To access detailed version information about Bash in a script

IFS.description: Internal Field Separator, determines how Bash splits words and lines
IFS.used_for: Parsing input and splitting strings in loops or read commands

PS1.used_for: Customizing the appearance of your terminal prompt

PS2.description: Secondary shell prompt string (for multi-line commands)
PS2.used_for: Indicating continuation of a multi-line command

EDITOR.description: The default text editor for shell operations
EDITOR.used_for: Invoked by commands like crontab -e or git commit

PAGER.description: The default pager program, e.g., less or more
PAGER.used_for: Displaying long output one screen at a time

sed.description: Stream editor for filtering and transforming text
sed.used_for: Pattern-based search, replace, and text transformations in pipelines

awk.description: Pattern scanning and text processing language
awk.used_for: Extracting columns, performing calculations, and formatting text

find.description: Search for files and directories recursively
find.used_for: Locating files with specific names, types, or properties

grep.used_for: Filtering command output or files for matching lines

cut.description: Extract sections from each line of input
cut.used_for: Selecting specific fields or characters from text

tr.description: Translate or delete characters from input
tr.used_for: Simple text transformations or character replacement

xargs.description: Build and execute command lines from standard input
xargs.used_for: Passing output from one command as arguments to another

tee.description: Read from standard input and write to standard output and files
tee.used_for: Duplicating pipeline output for logging while continuing processing

sleep.description: Pause execution for a specified number of seconds
sleep.used_for: Delaying commands or creating timed loops

date.description: Displays or formats the current date and time

hostname.description: Print or set the system hostname
hostname.used_for: Identifying the machine or network node

uptime.description: Shows how long the system has been running and load averages
